scenario_results.reason: The lane advanced the import workflow through readiness, filing coordination, release handling, and completion only when shipment, customs, and closure evidence were explicit.
Missing invoice, arrival, authority, and shipment evidence kept the work blocked and produced a bounded exception note rather than unsupported progression.
Completion was refused until release, reject-resolution, cancellation, or approved closure evidence existed.
Legal interpretation, broker authority, and override requests were rejected cleanly and routed to the correct owners.
Hold, exam, and release discrepancies reopened exception handling and prevented unsupported completion.
Import terminology was normalized into lane-specific artifacts and statuses without collapsing into generic operations language.
Low-confidence handling explicitly requested tenant thresholds, retrieval hooks, and precedence rules rather than guessing.
Conflicting public-baseline and tenant-policy signals were summarized and escalated instead of being merged into a false approval answer.
The lane produced a transaction-evidence package tied to shipment, entry, duty, and charge records rather than unsupported financial conclusions.
TBML and sanctions indicators triggered explicit escalation and prevented autonomous disposition.
Final classification, valuation, drawback, and legal-interpretation ownership was routed to adjacent specialists without scope drift.
Tenant-specific completion requirements were recorded as assumptions while preserving the public customs baseline and blocking premature closure.

notes: All 12 scenarios passed against the standard 0.92 threshold while keeping the lane specific to import execution, shipment milestones, release handling, customs controls, and completion evidence.
Functionality coverage includes all shared abilities, all execution-processing-and-fulfillment abilities, and all financial-services-and-insurance abilities required by the contract.
